flashcards.front: What is the core problem blockchain aims to solve?
flashcards.back: Enabling trustless, peer-to-peer transactions without a trusted central intermediary.
A logical dilemma illustrating the difficulty of achieving consensus in a distributed system when some components are faulty or malicious.

flashcards.front: According to the CAP theorem, which two properties does a partition-tolerant blockchain trade off?
flashcards.back: Consistency and Availability. Blockchains are typically AP systems (Available & Partition-Tolerant).

flashcards.front: What are the three main tiers of blockchain networks?
Name three key features of blockchain technology.
flashcards.back: Decentralization, Immutability, Transparency, Security, Consensus-Based (Any three are valid).
